REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS CONTINUED TO MAY 3, 2024, ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED, WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. ALL THE AUTHORITIES CITED BY THE PARTIES PROVIDE THAT WHEN A PARTY CHALLENGE AS ERRONEOUS THE METHODOLOGY THAT AN ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD USES, THE PETITIONER WAS ALSO SEEKING A REFUND OF TAXES PAID. (MAIN & VON KARMAN ASSOCIATES V. COUNTY OF ORANGE (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 337, 339, 342.) HERE, WHILE THE PETITIONER CHALLENGES THE METHODOLOGY USED BY THE ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, THE PETITION DOES NOT SEEK A REFUND OF REAL PROPERTY TAXES, AND THUS HAS NOT STATED A CLAIM UPON WHICH RELIEF CAN BE GRANTED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. AS THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED THE ONLY PROCEDURAL MECHANISM TO SEEK THE REQUESTED RELIEF BY THE PETITIONER IT HAS TO BE MADE IN CONJUNCTION WITH A REQUEST FOR REFUND. ADDITIONALLY, THE COURT FINDS THAT THE TIMING OF THE FILING OF THE WRIT UNDER THE CIRCUMSTANCES OF THIS CASE IS GOVERNED BY CAL. CODE REGS., TIT. 18, 325. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF MUST IN GOOD FAITH ALLEGE: (1) THAT PLAINTIFF COMPLIED WITH CAL. CODE REGS., TIT. 18, 325, SUBD. (A)(3) ("IF SO REQUESTED BY AN APPLICANT OR AN APPLICANT'S AGENT, THE DETERMINATION SHALL BECOME FINAL UPON ISSUANCE OF THE FINDINGS OF FACT. SUCH A REQUEST MUST BE MADE BY THE APPLICANT OR THE APPLICANT'S AGENT PRIOR TO OR AT THE CONCLUSION OF THE HEARING.") AND (2) THAT PLAINTIFF PAID TAXES AND COMPLIED WITH ADMINISTRATIVE PROCEDURES REGARDING THE REFUND FOR 2021, 2022, 2023, AND 2024. IF PLAINTIFF TAKES THE POSITION ADVANCED IN THE OPPOSITION THAT THIS IS NOT AN ACTION FOR REFUND AND THAT THE ISSUE IS NOT "RIPE," THE AMENDMENT TO CONFORM TO THE PROCEDURES UNDER MAIN & VON KARMAN ASSOCIATES V. COUNTY OF ORANGE (1994) 23 CAL.APP.4TH 337, IS NOT MADE IN GOOD FAITH. IF PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO AMEND IN ACCORDANCE WITH THIS RULING, ALL AMENDMENTS MUST BE MADE IN GOOD FAITH. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION IS SUSTAINED. DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR TAX REFUND FOR 2021-2024 IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO ALLEGE EXHAUSTION OF ADMINISTRATIVE REMEDIES IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE PROCEDURE OUTLINED IN DEFENDANT'S MOVING PAPERS AND FAILED TO PROVIDE ANY INDICATION THAT PLAINTIFF MAY IN GOOD FAITH AMEND THE PETITION TO ALLEGE COMPLIANCE. ALLEGATIONS IN PARAGRAPH 48 AND 49 SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF DID NOT COMPLY WITH THE PROCEDURE, EVEN THOUGH PLAINTIFF MAKES CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS THAT PLAINTIFF DID SO. DEMURRER TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR MANDAMUS IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO ASSERT A CLAIM FOR TAX REFUND FOR THE YEAR 2020 AND NAME CCSF AS A DEFENDANT ON THAT CAUSE OF ACTION. PETITIONER SUFFICIENTLY STATED FACTS SHOWING THAT THE CLAIM RELATED TO TAX YEAR 2020 WAS TIMELY MADE AFTER THE ISSUANCE OF THE FINDINGS OF FACT. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLE F. HAINES; CLERK: VANESSA WU; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion Requesting Certification Of Controlling Question Of Law For Appeal
Off Calendar Sep-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Samuel C. Ray appeared for petitioner Douglas Alan Greenberg (via Zoom).

Not contested. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENTS' MOTION REQUESTING CERTIFICATION OF CONTROLLING QUESTION OF LAW FOR APPEAL is DENIED. The Court will not exercise its discretion to certify that substantial grounds for difference of opinion exist regarding "the questions of whether the Demurrer Order erred in its interpretation of Rule 325(a)(3) and whether Petitioner's tax refund claim against CCSF is time-barred as "controlling question[s] of law as to which there are substantial grounds for difference of opinion."

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

Notice Of Motion Requesting Certification Of Controlling Question Of Law For Appeal

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENT'S MOTION TO STRIKE

Appearances: Thomas Lakritz of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4628) for respondents and moving parties; Samuel Ray of Coll & Ray LLP (415.579.1413) for petitioner and responding party.

Discussed. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DENIED. The Court has twice ruled that Petitioner may seek a writ of mandate and a refund simultaneously. The Court's May 24, 2024 ruling stated "Based on the authority provided by the parties, when a petitioner challenged the assessment appeals board's methodology as erroneous, the petitioner was also seeking a refund of taxes paid. (Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, 339, 342.)" Subsequently, the Court's September 19, 2024 order stated that "the Court previously ruled the only procedural mechanism to seek the requested relief by the Petitioner [] has to be made in conjunction with a request for refund." In short, Petitioner's writ is appropriate because it prays for a rehearing following the Board's errors of law, as opposed to lodging a challenge on the merits that would require the Board to make a specific assessment. The latter writ would be considered an impermissible tax refund action under Jefferson because there is another adequate remedy at law. (Compare William Jefferson & Co., Inc. v. Orange County Assessment Appeals Bd. No. 2 (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 1, 12-13, and Little v. Los Angeles County Assessment Appeals Bds. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 915, 919-922, with Von Karman, 23 Cal.App.4th at 337, 342-344). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jul-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RESPONDENTS' DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE

Appearances: Thomas Lakritz of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.4628) for respondents and moving parties; Samuel Ray of Coll & Ray LLP (415.579.1413) for petitioner and responding party.

Argued and the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: OVERRULED. The Court has twice ruled that Petitioner may seek a writ of mandate and a refund simultaneously. The Court's May 24, 2024 ruling stated "Based on the authority provided by the parties, when a petitioner challenged the assessment appeals board's methodology as erroneous, the petitioner was also seeking a refund of taxes paid. (Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, 339, 342.)" Subsequently, the Court's September 19, 2024 order stated that "the Court previously ruled the only procedural mechanism to seek the requested relief by the Petitioner [] has to be made in conjunction with a request for refund." In short, Petitioner's writ is appropriate because it prays for a rehearing following the Board's errors of law, as opposed to lodging a challenge on the merits that would require the Board to make a specific assessment. The latter writ would be considered an impermissible tax refund action under Jefferson because there is another adequate remedy at law. (Compare William Jefferson & Co., Inc. v. Orange County Assessment Appeals Bd. No. 2 (2014) 228 Cal.App.4th 1, 12-13, and Little v. Los Angeles County Assessment Appeals Bds. (2007) 155 Cal.App.4th 915, 919-922, with Von Karman, 23 Cal.App.4th at 337, 342-344).
Additionally, the statute of limitations began running on October 2, 2023. That was implicit in the Court's September 19, 2024 order, which held that "Petitioner sufficiently stated facts showing that the claim related to tax year 2020 was timely made after the issuance of the findings of fact." Nothing in the language of the statute requires the Petitioner to expressly toll the finality of the decision. It states that finding of fact need to be requested and "if so requested" the determination shall become final. The placement of the phrase "if so requested" makes it applicable to the phrase "findings of fact are requested" provision and not to the phrase "the determination shall become final upon issues of the finding of fact." Mr. Lakritz indicates he will make a written request that the Court certify the question to the Court of Appeal. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jan-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances

Luke Garvey, Colla & Ray LLP, 415-579-1414, via zoom for petitioner
Thomas Larkitz, Deputy City Attorney, 415-554-6547, via zoom for respondent

Mr. Lakritz contests to the tentative. After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO AMENED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED. Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for tax refund for 2021-2024 is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff failed to allege exhaustion of administrative remedies in accordance with the procedure outlined in Defendant's moving papers and failed to provide any indication that Plaintiff may in good faith amend the Petition to allege compliance. Allegations in paragraph 48 and 49 show that Plaintiff did not comply with the procedure, even though Plaintiff makes conclusory allegations that Plaintiff did so. Demurrer to the causes of action for mandamus is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to assert a claim for tax refund for the year 2020 and name CCSF as a defendant on that cause of action. Petitioner sufficiently stated facts showing that the claim related to tax year 2020 was timely made after the issuance of the findings of fact.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge Charle F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Sep-12-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Petition for Writ of Mandate is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. As the Court previously ruled the only procedural mechanism to seek the requested relief by the Petitioner it has to be made in conjunction with a request for refund. Additionally, the Court finds that the timing of the filing of the writ under the circumstances of this case is governed by Cal. Code Regs., tit. 18, 325. Therefore, Plaintiff must in good faith allege: (1) that Plaintiff complied with Cal. Code Regs., tit. 18, 325, subd. (a)(3) ("If so requested by an applicant or an applicant's agent, the determination shall become final upon issuance of the findings of fact. Such a request must be made by the applicant or the applicant's agent prior to or at the conclusion of the hearing.") and (2) that Plaintiff paid taxes and complied with administrative procedures regarding the refund for 2021, 2022, 2023, and 2024. If plaintiff takes the position advanced in the opposition that this is not an action for refund and that the issue is not "ripe," the amendment to conform to the procedures under Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, is not made in good faith. If Plaintiff intends to amend in accordance with this ruling, all amendments must be made in good faith.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

No appearances. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: is continued to August 28, 2024 for Petitioner to submit a supplemental brief limited to citations (no discussion or argument) to cases supporting Petitioner's proposition that the statute of limitations starts running from the October 2, 2023 decision "because the April 28, 2023 decision letter did not state the Respondent's reasoning because this [October 2, 2023] was the first time Respondent outlined its reasoning, which revealed its procedural and legal errors." The cases must be factually similar (written notice of decision followed by issuance of findings of fact; and a superior court case filed outside of 90 days after the written notice of decision). If Petitioner cannot locate any such cases, the supplemental brief should be filed limited to the statement that no factually similar cases could be found. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar May-24-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

RESPONDENT ASSESSMENT APPEALS BOARD OF THE CITY AND COUNTY DEMURRER to PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is SUSTAINED, with leave to amend. All the authorities cited by the parties provide that when a party challenge as erroneous the methodology that an assessment appeals board uses, the petitioner was also seeking a refund of taxes paid. (Main & Von Karman Associates v. County of Orange (1994) 23 Cal.App.4th 337, 339, 342.) Here, while the Petitioner challenges the methodology used by the Assessment Appeals Board of the City and County of San Francisco, the Petition does not seek a refund of real property taxes, and thus has not stated a claim upon which relief can be granted.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
